title: Release Schedule | U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA)
summary: This page is the official BEA release schedule for 2026, listing upcoming dates and times for macroeconomic data releases including GDP estimates, Personal Income and Outlays, and International Trade statistics. No actual data or numbers are provided—only the calendar of future release events through December 2026.
key_points: The BEA publishes GDP estimates in three stages: Advance Estimate, Second Estimate, and Third Estimate, each with distinct release dates throughout 2026.
Personal Income and Outlays reports are scheduled monthly, typically released on the last business day of each month.
U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services data is released monthly, with an Annual Update scheduled for June 9, 2026.
Key GDP release dates in 2026: April 30 (Q1 Advance), July 30 (Q2 Advance), October 29 (Q3 Advance).
Additional scheduled releases include Foreign Direct Investment, U.S. Transactions and Investment Position, and State-level GDP data.
Some releases are marked 'To Be Announced' including Trade in Value Added (Spring 2026) and Outdoor Recreation Economic Statistics (2026).
why_it_matters: This schedule serves as a macro catalyst calendar for market participants. Knowing the exact dates and times of BEA releases (GDP, Personal Income and Outlays, Trade data) allows traders and analysts to prepare for potential market-moving events. Personal Income and Outlays includes PCE inflation data, a key Fed inflation gauge. GDP releases provide comprehensive economic growth readings.
macro_context: The schedule shows a regular cadence of critical macro releases. GDP data is released three times per quarter (Advance/Second/Third estimates). Personal Income and Outlays is released monthly and contains PCE price index data. International Trade data is released monthly with an annual revision in June. These releases collectively form the backbone of U.S. economic data reporting and inform Fed policy decisions.

issues_found: PAGE_TYPE is listing/schedule page, not an actual data release - source is a release calendar listing future events
No actual data, numbers, or substantive economic content present - only dates and titles of future releases
event_type 'data_release_other' is misleading - this is not a data release
Source has thin archival value; market impact only materializes when actual releases occur
Rules specify: reject if source is a homepage, navigation, listing, or search page - a release schedule is functionally a listing page

notes: Source is a release schedule rather than a data release itself. Contains no numerical data—only dates, times, and titles of future BEA releases. Relevant for identifying macro catalyst timing but thin on substantive content. PUBLISHED_AT field is empty per source metadata. Schedule appears current as it covers April 2026 onward, consistent with TODAY of 2026-03-31.
